questions.q: In which year was the Hindi film Mother India released?
questions.explanation: Mother India was released in 1957.

questions.q: Who directed the 1957 epic film Mother India?
questions.explanation: Mother India was directed by Mehboob Khan under his banner Mehboob Productions.

questions.q: Which actress played the lead role of Radha in Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Nargis played Radha, the central peasant woman, in Mother India.

questions.q: What is the name of the central female character played by Nargis in Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Nargis portrayed Radha, a poor peasant woman who anchors the film's story.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) tells the story of a woman from which section of rural society?
questions.explanation: The film centres on Radha, a poor peasant woman struggling against poverty and hardship.

questions.q: In Mother India (1957), what does Radha ultimately do to her rebellious outlaw son Birju to uphold the village's honour?
options: She exiles him from the village
She hands him to the police
questions.explanation: Radha shoots and kills her own son Birju to stop him from dishonouring a village woman.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) was India's official entry and first nomination for which international award?
options: The Golden Globe for Best Picture
The Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film
questions.explanation: Mother India was the first Indian film nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

questions.q: In which language was the 1957 film Mother India made?
questions.explanation: Mother India is a Hindi-language film.

questions.q: Which actor played the rebellious son Birju in Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Sunil Dutt played Birju, the angry younger son who becomes an outlaw.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) is widely regarded as a landmark of which industry?
questions.explanation: Mother India is considered one of the most iconic and enduring classics of Indian cinema.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) was a reworking and grander remake of which earlier Mehboob Khan film?
questions.explanation: Mother India was a remake of Mehboob Khan's own 1940 film Aurat.

questions.q: Who composed the music for the 1957 film Mother India?
questions.explanation: The music for Mother India was composed by Naushad.

questions.q: Which actor played the role of Shamu, Radha's husband, in Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Raaj Kumar played Shamu, Radha's husband, who loses his arms in a farming accident and leaves the family.

questions.q: Which actor played the exploitative moneylender Sukhilala in Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Kanhaiyalal played the greedy moneylender Sukhilala who torments the village.

questions.q: Which actor, who played Birju in Mother India (1957), later married his co-star Nargis?
questions.explanation: Sunil Dutt married Nargis after the film; he is said to have rescued her from a fire on the set during shooting.

questions.q: At which 1958 Academy Awards ceremony did Mother India compete for the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar?
questions.explanation: Mother India competed at the 30th Academy Awards, held in 1958.

questions.q: Which Italian film famously defeated Mother India for the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar by a reported single vote?
questions.explanation: Federico Fellini's Nights of Cabiria won the Oscar, narrowly beating Mother India.

questions.q: Which natural disaster is a key turning point in the rural saga of Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: A catastrophic flood destroys the village's crops and deepens Radha's struggle in the film.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) is often read as an allegory in which the central mother figure symbolizes what?
questions.explanation: Radha is widely interpreted as an embodiment of the newly independent Indian nation, a self-sacrificing mother.

questions.q: Which acclaimed song picturised on Nargis features in Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: "Duniya Mein Hum Aaye Hain" is one of the well-known songs from Mother India.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) won the All India Certificate of Merit and was a major recipient at which national award body of its era?
questions.explanation: Mother India was honoured at the National Film Awards, where it received the All India Certificate of Merit, and at the Filmfare Awards, where it won Best Film.

questions.q: At the 1958 Filmfare Awards, which performer from Mother India won the Best Actress trophy?
questions.explanation: Nargis won the Filmfare Award for Best Actress for her portrayal of Radha.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) was one of the earliest major Hindi films shot in which colour process?
questions.explanation: Mother India was filmed in Technicolor, lending its rural landscapes a vivid, epic look.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer for the visually striking 1957 film Mother India?
questions.explanation: Faredoon A. Irani was the cinematographer of Mother India, a long-time collaborator of Mehboob Khan.

questions.q: Which actor played Ramu, the elder and more obedient son, in Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Kumar played Ramu, the dutiful elder son, contrasting with Sunil Dutt's rebellious Birju.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's production banner, which made Mother India (1957), used a distinctive logo featuring what symbol?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Productions' logo featured a hammer and sickle, set against the couplet "Muddai laakh bura chaahe to kya hota hai."

questions.q: In the framing device of Mother India (1957), the story is told as a flashback when the elderly Radha is asked to do what?
options: Receive an award in the city
Cast the first vote in an election
questions.explanation: The film opens with the aged Radha being asked to inaugurate a new canal, prompting her recollection of her life.

questions.q: Mother India (1957) is noted as having been one of the most expensive Indian films produced up to which point in time?
questions.explanation: Mother India was one of the most expensive Indian films produced up to its 1957 release.

questions.q: The 2001 Bollywood film Lagaan is sometimes compared to Mother India because both became which kind of Indian milestone?
options: The first Indian films shot abroad
Indian films nominated for the Best Foreign Language Film Oscar
questions.explanation: Like Mother India, Lagaan (2001) earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Foreign Language Film.

questions.q: Which real-life family relationship connects Nargis and Sunil Dutt, the co-stars of Mother India (1957), to later Hindi cinema?
options: They were the parents of actor Sanjay Dutt
questions.explanation: Nargis and Sunil Dutt married after the film and became the parents of actor Sanjay Dutt.
